title: Life Biosciences Phase 1 ER-100 trial: first patient dosed with controlled OSK epigenetic reprogramming therapy
description: Life Biosciences confirmed the first participant in its NCT07290244 Phase 1 trial of ER-100 has been dosed, using OCT4/SOX2/KLF4 transcription-factor gene therapy controlled by an oral doxycycline on/off switch. The eye-targeted approach (glaucoma, NAION) is engineered to limit cancer risk seen in past reprogramming work, with under 20 patients enrolling across Boston, NYC, LA, and Charleston sites.

title: Albert Einstein College of Medicine launches BIO-VITAL gerotherapeutics validation program
description: Einstein's Institute for Geroscience launched the Batia and Idan Ofer BIO-VITAL program offering biotech and pharma partners 30+ assays across cellular aging, preclinical aging models, and human longevity multi-omics cores (led by Cuervo, Huffman, Barzilai, Milman). The Ofer Family Foundation-backed initiative gives industry direct access to centenarian datasets and proprietary aging models for blinded drug testing and biomarker discovery.

title: Continuous low-dose rapamycin selectively attenuates IL-17 γδ T-cells and neuroinflammation in aged mice
description: PLOS ONE paper covered by Fight Aging shows continuous low-dose dietary rapamycin in aged mice did not broadly suppress innate/adaptive immunity but selectively reduced age-associated IL-17-producing γδ T cells in the peritoneal cavity and attenuated microglia inflammatory response to endotoxin challenge. This refines the dose-response argument for daily versus weekly rapamycin protocols in humans.

title: UT San Antonio launches multi-phase rapamycin clinical study in older adults
description: UT Health San Antonio researchers, led by Dean Kellogg Jr., MD/PhD (Division of Geriatrics, Gerontology and Palliative Medicine), have launched a multi-phase clinical study to characterize rapamycin's biological effects in older adults. Combined with the new Stanfield-Kaeberlein trial and PEARL data, the field now has three distinct active rapamycin programs in humans.

title: XPrize Healthspan: Sinclair targets $101M grand prize with oral cellular-reprogramming drug
description: In coverage of the Life Biosciences first-in-human dose, David Sinclair publicly announced plans to develop an oral version of his three-factor reprogramming therapy to compete for the $101M XPrize Healthspan grand prize, which requires reversing patient biological age by 10 years in under one year. This sets an explicit translational target for the oral reprogramming class.

title: Life Biosciences announces first patient dosed in Phase 1 ER-100 trial
description: Phase 1 trial (NCT07290244) for ER-100 in glaucoma and NAION dosed first participant; epigenetic restoration platform uses OCT4/SOX2/KLF4 with doxycycline on/off control, eight-week activation window.

title: World-first: therapy to make cells young again trialled in a person
description: Nature feature describes Life Biosciences' first patient dose, with Matt Kaeberlein noting the eye is a wise first organ due to lower catastrophic-side-effect risk versus other tissues.

title: First Human Receives Experimental Therapy to Reverse Cellular Aging
description: Coverage details ER-100's three-factor approach (skipping the fourth Yamanaka factor most associated with cancer) and the doxycycline-controlled on/off switch limiting off-target reprogramming risk.

title: The first-ever reverse-aging drug was just injected into a human
description: Under 20 patients are enrolling at Boston, NYC, LA, and Charleston sites; the six-month observation window will be the field's first clean read on safety and efficacy in humans.

title: Daily briefing: Trial to 'de-age' cells treats first person
description: Nature daily briefing summarizes Life Biosciences' first human dose as a defining moment for partial-reprogramming therapeutics.

title: First human trial of reverse-aging drug begins
description: Mainstream business coverage of the ER-100 milestone framing it as the biggest tangible step yet for the longevity industry.

title: Albert Einstein College of Medicine launches BIO-VITAL to accelerate gerotherapeutics
description: BIO-VITAL connects biotech and pharmaceutical partners with Einstein's aging research expertise; three integrated cores cover cellular hallmarks, preclinical models, and human longevity multi-omics.

title: Low-dose continuous rapamycin favorably alters the aging immune system
description: PLOS ONE study shows prolonged low-dose dietary rapamycin in mice selectively reduced IL-17-producing γδ T cells and microglial inflammatory phenotype after endotoxin challenge without broad immunosuppression.

title: UT San Antonio launches multi-phase rapamycin study in older adults
description: Dean Kellogg Jr. (UT San Antonio Geriatrics) leads multi-phase clinical study to understand rapamycin's biological effects in older adults; covered by Texas Public Radio.

title: Rejuvenate Bio announces $6M financing and strategic R&D collaboration with Merck Animal Health
description: $6M round led by VCapital with Merck Animal Health, Kendall Capital Partners, Connecticut Innovations and Digitalis funding continued development of Rejuvenate Bio's gene-therapy platform.

title: 60 Minutes: Dog Aging Project rapamycin trial could unlock secrets for people
description: NIH-funded large-scale rapamycin RCT in pet dogs; hundreds dosed in the active arm with healthspan endpoints; mainstream coverage shifts public conversation.

title: PEARL trial: rapamycin slows muscle and bone aging in first long-term human RCT
description: 115 adults 50-85 on 10 mg/week rapamycin for 48 weeks: muscle preservation in women, bone preservation in men, no serious adverse events.

title: Stanfield-Kaeberlein rapamycin RCT: 6 mg/week sirolimus vs placebo on strength and endurance
description: BMC-registered double-blind RCT in 40 adults aged 65-80 over 13 weeks measures strength and endurance as primary endpoints; next major translational checkpoint for low-dose rapamycin.

title: Nature 2026 master review: therapeutic strategies for aging and age-associated diseases
description: Comprehensive 2026 review integrating senolytics, senomorphics, senoreversion, metabolic/dietary interventions, biomarkers, multiomics and AI into one unified geroscience framework.

title: Sinclair + Tally Health publish first comprehensive evidence map of human epigenetic aging interventions
description: Frontiers in Genetics review identifies exercise, semaglutide, pitavastatin, plant-rich diet, caloric restriction, omega-3, multivitamin as interventions associated with favorable next-gen epigenetic clock changes (41 human studies).

title: UCSD: Semaglutide may slow biological aging in randomized HIV lipohypertrophy trial
description: First RCT showing semaglutide slowed DunedinPACE by 9% and PCGrimAge mortality clock relative to placebo over 32 weeks; published in Nature Communications.

title: NewLimit raises $435M Series C at $3.1B valuation
description: Founders Fund-led round (Thrive, Lilly Ventures, Friedman, Gross) brings Brian Armstrong-founded cell-reprogramming company to $3.1B valuation ahead of first liver age-reversal human trial.

title: Forever Healthy Foundation launches Evipedia.ai — open encyclopedia for 500+ longevity interventions
description: Michael Greve's Forever Healthy Foundation releases Evipedia.ai with in-depth evidence reviews of 500+ health and longevity interventions including first-generation rejuvenation therapies, supplements, and protocols.

title: Cross-species transcriptomic aging clock published in Nature
description: Universal aging signature across mice, monkeys and humans (>11,000 gene activity samples) identifies CDKN1A and LGALS3 as markers tracking mortality and multiple diseases in UK Biobank data.

title: Longevity Technology Week 23: Ōura Ring 5 launches; Elysium opens TELI longevity clinic
description: Ōura Ring 5 (40% smaller, BP + breathing + GLP-1 insights); Elysium opens The Elysium Longevity Institute with physician oversight, testing, supplements, prescriptions, and peptides.

title: Optispan/Kaeberlein: 'Aging is a Disease. We Just Don't Know How to Define It Yet'
description: Longevity scientist panel discussing Phase 1 oral small-molecule trial for Alzheimer's (BBB-penetrant), autophagy-based mechanisms, and field's framing of aging as a disease.

title: Sirolimus available at TrumpRx at $0.66/mg
description: Generic sirolimus listed on TrumpRx.gov at $0.66/mg — material pricing development for off-label longevity users on weekly rapamycin protocols.

title: Thirty years after the discovery of SA-β-gal — Aging review
description: Aging Volume 18 review 'Blue period – features of senescence 30 years after beta-galactosidase' covers three decades of senescence biomarker discovery and methodology.

title: Has humanity tested its first true anti-aging drug?
description: Social commentary on the Life Biosciences first dose emphasizing cellular senescence as a fundamental driver of aging and cautioning that safety, efficacy and meaningful rejuvenation remain to be demonstrated.

title: D+Q senolytic causes brain myelin damage in mice — UConn safety warning
description: UConn researchers show dasatinib + quercetin at standard longevity doses triggers significant demyelination in corpus callosum white matter in young and old mice — urgent caution on off-label human use.
